<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
 I'm clarifying the scope for S6—it should cover the HistoryList component and history page with tests T26, T27, T28, and T39, while T40-T42 are manual checklists that belong to S8 and fall outside what I need to implement here. I'll focus on T26, T27, T28 (the HistoryList portion), and T39, noting that T40–T42 are S8 manual checklists outside this scope. My plan is to create the HistoryList component as a sync presentational component and the history page as an async Server Component, then write tests covering empty and populated states, signpost presence, framing notice placement, and accessibility requirements like semantic `<dl>` structure and touch target sizing.

For the populated state, each row will display an ISO timestamp with a human-readable relative phrase, disposable income and band chip (both hidden for empty rows), outcome state label, and a collapsible details section with an income breakdown. The SupportSignpost will appear once at the bottom using the latest outcome state for populated lists or "no-data" for empty ones, followed by the FramingNotice. I'll write a small helper function to generate the relative time phrase since we don't have date-fns as a dependency, and structure the list semantically with `<ol>` and `<li>` elements wrapping the time, income, and collapsible details.

For the test-driven approach, I'll write failing tests first to verify the spec requirements—including that disposable income and band chip are suppressed for no-data rows, that the summary text shows "Show what was submitted on <date>", and that both the dashboard and history views include the FramingNotice. Then I'll implement the HistoryList component and history page, run the tests to iterate, and finish with linting and type checking.</details></think>

## Plan (auditable, before any code)

### Scope: tech-spec **S6 — History view**

**Upstream refs re-read:**

- **PRD:** R3 (return later & view prior snapshots), R7 (signpost on every outcome surface), R18 (a11y WCAG 2.2 AA), R20 (reflection-not-advice framing on every outcome screen).
- **TECH_SPEC S6:** `src/app/history/page.tsx` (async I/O wrapper) + `components/HistoryList.tsx` (sync, props-only). Newest-first list; empty state with CTA to `/dashboard/update`; `<ol>` of rows each containing `<time dateTime>`, disposable income, band chip (suppressed for no-data), outcome state label, `<details>/<summary>` disclosure with `<dl>` of IE breakdown; `<SupportSignpost />` and `<FramingNotice />` rendered **inside** the component (both empty and populated states), in that order at the bottom.
- **TEST_PLAN** — the four `T*` whose `Touches:` includes `S6`:
  - **T26** (R3, R7, R18, R20; S6, S9) — empty + populated render contract + axe clean both states.
  - **T27** (R7; S6) — signpost ubiquity, non-empty link in both states.
  - **T28 history half** (R20; S4, S6, S9) — `<FramingNotice />` present when rendering `<HistoryList />` standalone (dashboard half already shipped in S014).
  - **T39** (R18; S6) — populated axe smoke, `<dl>` semantics, summary meets 24 CSS-px target.

**Out of scope for this slice** (call-outs to keep guardrails honest):

- **T40 / T41 / T42** — manual integration checklists for README / DECISIONS / prompt history; they are owned by **S8**, not S6 (test-plan §7 traceability rows pin them to `S8`). I'm not implementing S8 here. I'll note them in the session snapshot as deliberately deferred.
- The async `src/app/history/page.tsx` itself is **not** unit-tested (page-vs-component split, tech-spec §4 cross-cutting). The Vitest surface is the sync `<HistoryList />` only.

### Files I will add / edit

| File | Why |
|---|---|
| `tests/s6/t26-history-list-states.test.tsx` | T26 (empty + populated + axe both) |
| `tests/s6/t27-signpost-ubiquity.test.tsx` | T27 (non-empty signpost link in both states) |
| `tests/s6/t28-framing-ubiquity-history.test.tsx` | T28 history half |
| `tests/s6/t39-history-list-a11y.test.tsx` | T39 (axe, `<dl>` semantics, summary target size) |
| `components/HistoryList.tsx` | The sync presentational component |
| `src/app/history/page.tsx` | Thin async I/O wrapper |

No edits to anything outside this slice (no formatting sweeps, no dep bumps, no opportunistic refactors). The existing `<SupportSignpost />` and `<FramingNotice />` from S4/S9 are imported as-is.

**Async I/O wrapper** (not unit-tested per tech-spec §4 page-vs-component split):

| T28 (history half) | `tests/s6/t28-framing-ubiquity-history.test.tsx` | `<FramingNotice />` is in the document when `<HistoryList />` is rendered standalone, in both states |

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

The [critic review](a3f371ef-9319-40c3-ba92-9d40dc93170b) returned **Verdict: Ship as-is**, with no must-fix findings. One Medium item, seven Low items, and three Suspicion flags — all clearly tied to either spec interpretation (judgement calls already recorded in S017's deviations table) or known coverage limits the TEST_PLAN itself flags.

### Highlights

**Top-3 must-fix: empty.** The critic explicitly declined to produce three even as a courtesy. The slice is committable as-is.

**The one Medium I want you to see before committing — F-M1:**

```155:157:components/HistoryList.tsx
    <li
      aria-label={`Snapshot from ${formattedDate}`}
      className="rounded-lg border border-foreground/15 p-4"
    >
```

`aria-label` on a non-interactive `<li>` becomes the row's accessible name, which can cause some screen readers to summarise the row as just *"Snapshot from 10 June 2026"* and skip the descendant disposable / band-chip / outcome-state text. Spec line 327 ("each row is an `<li>` with a programmatic name") is loose enough to accept either approach. A safer rewrite would be `aria-labelledby` pointing at the in-row `<time>` element, or dropping the attribute and letting the visible text serve as the accname. The fix is a single attribute change and would not touch any test.

I'm flagging it here rather than applying it because:

1. It is **defensible per spec** (the spec asked for a programmatic name; this delivers one).
2. **No `T*` asserts on it**, and axe does not flag the pattern.
3. The S017 snapshot D-86 already records it as a known judgement call.

If you want to apply the safer pattern as a one-line fix in this session, I'll do it now. Otherwise I'll leave it for a future S6 amendment or the S8 documentation pass.

**Other findings worth knowing about but not blockers:**

- **L1** — Duplicate "Surplus / Breakeven / Shortfall" string per row (state label + band chip text are identical). Spec line 322 lists both as required, so it's spec-faithful redundancy.
- **L3** — `irregularIncomeNote` (Casey/A3) is not rendered on history rows even though it appears on the dashboard. Spec does not require it on history — recording as a future R9-amendment seam, not a current gap.
- **L7 / G2** — SC 2.5.8 (24-px target) is enforced via Tailwind class-name assertion on `<summary>` only; the empty-state CTA isn't asserted (though it does carry `min-h-6 min-w-6`). Acceptable in unit-test fidelity since jsdom doesn't lay out.
- **G1** — No `T*` asserts the per-row outcome state label is present (only that band chip + disposable income are suppressed for no-data). A future regression dropping the label wouldn't be auto-caught.

**Coverage gaps that are deliberate (not gaps):** relative phrase, `<li aria-label>`, 400% reflow — all justified in S017 or pinned to manual checks in TEST_PLAN §6.

**Scope discipline check: pass.** No edits outside the four S6 files. Page-vs-component split intact. Public contracts of S2/S4/S9 imports unchanged. Pre-existing lint nits left untouched.

**Deferred-items audit: claim supported.** T40/T41/T42 are pinned to S8 in both TEST_PLAN §3 and §7; pulling them into S6 would have been a workflow rule 3 gate-cross.

### What I'd do next

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

## Critic round closed — `Ship as-is` plus two applied fixes

### What I changed in response to the critic

**F-M1 (Medium, accessibility):** Swapped `<li aria-label="Snapshot from <date>">` for `<li aria-labelledby="<timeId> <stateLabelId>">`, where `timeId` and `stateLabelId` point at the in-row `<time>` element and the outcome state `<span>` respectively. The row's accessible name is now derived from its own descendant text ("10 June 2026 Surplus") rather than a string attribute that shadows it. Spec line 327's "programmatic name" commitment is preserved.

```155:170:components/HistoryList.tsx
    <li
      aria-labelledby={`${timeId} ${stateLabelId}`}
      className="rounded-lg border border-foreground/15 p-4"
    >
      <div className="flex flex-wrap items-baseline justify-between gap-2">
        <time
          id={timeId}
          dateTime={snapshot.takenAt}
          className="text-sm font-medium text-foreground"
        >
```

**G1 (Low, coverage):** Extended T26's populated-state test to assert the per-row outcome state label is present with the expected text per outcome state (`"Surplus"`, `"Shortfall"`, `"No data yet"`). The outcome state `<span>` gained a `data-testid="outcome-state-label"` for the test to target. A future regression dropping the label would now be auto-caught.

### Verification

- `npx vitest run tests/s6` — 9/9 pass (same 4 test files; T26 now does the extra assertion per row)
- `npm test` — **131/131** still pass
- `npm run typecheck` — clean
- `npx biome check tests/s6/ components/HistoryList.tsx src/app/history/page.tsx` — clean
- `npm run lint` — same three pre-existing S3/S4 formatter nits (untouched per scope rule)

### What I deliberately did **not** apply

Per the critic's own assessment ("none required for this slice to land"), these stay as recorded judgement calls in S017 D-91 rather than code changes:

- L1 (duplicate "Surplus" string between state label and band chip — spec line 322 lists both as required)
- L2 (empty-state region label noun mismatch — minor a11y polish)
- L3 (no `irregularIncomeNote` on history rows — spec does not require it; would need R9 amendment to add)
- L4 (non-deterministic relative phrase — by-design, justified in D-84)
- L5 (relative-phrase bucket arithmetic readability)
- L6 (unused `getPersonaById` return value — consistent with existing pattern in `src/app/dashboard/page.tsx`)
- L7 (SC 2.5.8 class-name vs computed-style assertion — jsdom doesn't lay out, manual reflow per TEST_PLAN §6)
- S1/S2/S3 (Suspicion items — judgement calls already recorded in D-83/D-91)

### Audit trail updated

- `docs/ai/sessions/S017-implement-s6.md` — D-91 added; "Files changed" table updated to flag the post-critic edits; "Tests run" notes the re-verification.
- `docs/PROMPT_HISTORY.md` — S017 row title becomes "History view (+ critic round)"; summary updated with the critic verdict and D-91 fixes.

Slice remains uncommitted on `main`. Ready for `/implement S8` when you are.
